properties.remark: REPORTED BY OBSERVER
1/2 TO 1 1/4 INCH. BROKEN WINDOWS, LOST POWER AND ROOF DAMAGE.
STATE POLICE REPORTED GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL ON INTERSTATE 25 AT MILE MARKER 92.
60 TO 70 MPH WINDS KNOCKED OUT POWER...TOPPLED TWO LARGE TREES AND CLOSED THE INSTERSTATE IN AND AROUND DEMING.
SPOTTER AT AIRFIELD JUST NORTH OF COLUMBUS.
MEASURED AT NWS OFFICE

THE NORTHEAST AND WEST PORTIONS OF EL PASO RECEIVED 2 TO ALMOST 3 INCHES OF RAIN WITHIN AN HOUR, RESULTING IN MAJOR STREET FLOODING AND FLOODING OF BUILDINGS. SEVERAL STREETS WERE CLOSED AND ONE APARTMENT COMPLEX WAS EVACUATED.
WATER RUNNING ACROSS BIA ROUTE 10 WITH ROCKS...LOGS... AND ASH. FEW RESIDENCES HAVE DRIVEWAYS BLOCKED BY RUNNING WATER. NOTE...DISTANCE FROM MESCALERO ESTIMATED.
EM REPORTED WATER ACROSS SH51.
KDMN ASOS GUST TO 65 KNOTS OR 74 MPH.
NUMEROUS ROADS FLOODED THROUGHOUT LUNA COUNTY. REPORTED BY LUNA CO ROAD DEPARTMENT.
SCATTERED POWER AND PHONE LINES DOWNED THROUGH OUT DEMING. FROM LUNA COUNTY DISPATCH. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED.
NUMEROUS REPORTS OF STREET FLOODING AROUND THE CITY...FROM CENTRAL DISPATCH.
DONA ANA COUNTY CENTRAL DISPATCH REPORTS MULTIPLE ROAD CLOSURES AND HOMES FLOODED.
FLOODING ON DONIPHAN DRIVE...EL PASO SHERIFFS OFFICE MAY CLOSE ROAD.
NUMEROUS ROCK SLIDES ON TRANSMOUNTAIN PASS...MOSTLY CONTAINED BY GUARDRAILS...BUT SOME ROCKS IN ROAD WAY. VISIBILITY VERY POOR DUE TO FOG...CLOUDS TOPPING FRANKLIN MOUNTAINS.
WATER KNEE DEEP IN FRONT YARDS.

FLOOD WATERS OVER-TOPPED CONTAINMENT PONDS AT SOUTH TULAROSA FIRE BURN SCAR AREA RESULTING IN WIDESPREAD FLASH FLOODING EVENT. PARTS OF US-70 SHUT DOWN...2 DAMS FAILED AT MUD CANYON NEAR SAN JUAN DRIVE AND NM ROUTE 244. ROAD TO MESCALERO FIRE STATION WASHED OUT... SEVERAL BUILDINGS AFFECTED BY FLOOD WATER AND SEVERAL SECONADRY ROADS CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER.
AT LEAST 5 ROAD CLOSURES THROUGHOUT THE CITY DUE TO FLOODING.
MULTIPLE LOW WATER CROSSINGS FLOODED. FOUR WHELL DRIVE ACCESS ONLY TO OTHER AREAS.
FLOODING WEST SIDE OF EL PASO WITH ROCKS ON RAOD. WATER RUNNING CURB TO CURB.
LAS CRUCES AIRPORT ASOS MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 50 KNOTS. TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN NEAR MAYFIELD HIGH SCHOOL.
SPOTTER RELAYED REPORT OF TREES DOWN ON A RANCH.
KVIA REPORTS 60 MPH GUST IN NORTHEAST EL PASO... TRANSMOUNTAIN AT RUSHING.
WATER RESCUE OF AKLAN HILL ROAD. AT LEAST 3 TO 4 INCHES OF WATER ON SECTIONS OF HIGHWAY 35 NORTH OF MIMBRES.

OVER 2 FEET OF WATER OVER HIGHWAY 27...COVERING AN AREA 25 FEET WIDE ACROSS THE ROAD.
3 FEET OF WATER OVER TOP OF ROAD BY SANTA CLARA CREEK.

EVACUATIONS DUE TO POSSIBLE FLOODING IN LAS PALOMAS.
WATER ESTIMATED AT 1 FOOT DEEP COVERING FULL WIDTH OF CUCHILLO CREEK LOW WATER CROSSING. BARRICADES ARE UP.
WATER GOING OVER BANKS OF PALOMAS CREEK IN A SOME PLACES. DRAINAGE IS INTO ADJACENT FIELDS IN THE COMMUNITIES OF LAS PALOMAS, CUCHILLO, WINTON AND MONTICELLO.
WATER OVER HIGHWAY 187 LOW WATER CROSSINGS JUST NORTH OF ARREY AT MILE MARKERS 15, 17 AND 20.
